---
title: "polyval"
---


```
y = polyval(p::AbstractArray, x::Union{AbstractArray, Number})
```

Evaluates the polynomial p at each point in x. The argument p is a vector of length n+1 whose
elements are the coefficients (in ascending order of powers) of an nth-degree polynomial:

$$p(x) = p[1] + p[2]x + p[3]x^2 + \ldots + p[n+1]x^n$$

The polynomial coefficients in p can be calculated by functions like {{< gmtref polyfit >}},
but you can specify any vector for the coefficients.

Examples
--------

Evaluate the polynomial $p(x)=3x^2 +2x + 1$ at the points x=5,7,9. The polynomial coefficients
can be represented by the vector [1, 2, 3].

```{julia}
using GMT

p = [1, 2, 3];
x = [5, 7, 9];
y = polyval(p, x)
```

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
using GMT
GMT.doc_source_links("polyval")
```


See Also
--------

{{< gmtref polyfit >}}, {{< gmtref plot >}}

- [Alphabetical Function List](../alphabetical.html)
